ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: O yɛlim ye m kemi o saam yidimin, nɛ banɛ aan o dɔɔg nidibin la bɔ pu'a na tis o biig la.”
translated: Oh friend, come here, if you want to go tonight when the sun sets, then come.

ref: Then he said, “Friends, please don't do such a terrible thing!
text: ka yɛli ba ye, “Ayei, m zuanama! Da tʋmi tʋʋmbɛ'ɛd kaŋaa!
translated: And they say, "Oh, my mother! Come give the gift now!"

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Duom giligi kaae gɔs teŋ la wa'alim nɛ di labisim ka m ye zaŋ nɛ tisif!”
translated: Give our gift to you and your people, the speakers of our language, that we may speak words of thanks!

ref: Before Lot and his guests could go to bed, every man in Sodom, young and old, came and stood outside his house
text: Ban da nan zin' sɔnsid la, Sodom dasam nɛ bʋnkʋd wʋsa da yi teŋin la na la'asi gilig yir la.
translated: But the people of that city, Sodom was destroyed and burned, and the people of this city were destroyed because of their sins.

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Ka o lɛn nɔk pʋʋg ya'as du'a biribiŋ ka yɛl ye, “Zugsɔb baŋ ye ba pʋ nɔŋi ma ka tisim oŋa ya'as.” Ka pʋd on ye Simeon.
translated: And the man who had a donkey's son said to Biribing, "Zugob, they said you have a donkey's son." And the father is Simeon.
